seoTitle: Blacksoil vs Blackswamp — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Blacksoil and Blackswamp. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Blacksoil and Blackswamp.
heading: Blacksoil vs Blackswamp — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Blacksoil, Queensland and Blackswamp, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Blacksoil has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Blackswamp (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Blacksoil or Blackswamp safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Blacksoil has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Blackswamp scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Blacksoil scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Blackswamp scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Blacksoil or Blackswamp?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Blacksoil has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
